finding: Cause: secondary to mild infantile form

differentials.name: nausea, intermittent vomiting, postprandial distress, heartburn
differentials.raw: nausea, intermittent vomiting, postprandial distress, heartburn

differentials.name: Peptic ulcer disease (in 50-74%) ← prolonged gastrin production ← stasis of food
differentials.raw: Peptic ulcer disease (in 50-74%) ← prolonged gastrin production ← stasis of food

context: Associated with: √  persistent elongation (2-4 cm) + concentric narrowing of pyloric channel √  parallel + preserved mucosal folds √  antispasmodics show no effect on narrowing √  proximal benign ulcer (74%), usually near incisura Focal Pyloric Hypertrophy =  TORUS HYPERPLASIA =  localized muscle hypertrophy on lesser curvature =  milder atypical form of HPS √  flattening of distal lesser curvature
